think you guys are making great content, that makes sense.
Like, I like the GrowthX content.
I like the brand feel of it.
I like the rest of these things.
But it just feels like we're not kind of aligned on, like, what does excellent look like here.

And it doesn't feel like there's anyone on GrowthX side that's, like, really, like, owning it to, like, hey, we're on, like, I guess, like, the thing I feel is, like, like, would it make me feel different in this call?
It'd be, like, hey, look, it's only 275, but actually, like, like, we see these promising signs, and actually we think by, like, March we can be here.
And then we go hit.
Because it's not like I expect everything to be up and to the right.
It's more that I kind of feel like we should be going for something kind of aggressive, which is, like, we want search to be adding a ton within six months, I think, was the original goal.
Yeah.
And right now it doesn't feel like we're close to that, so.
Yeah, understood, can certainly take that back.
You know, I'll say like Katya did come to this just to be fair, like, and had asked for the projections.
I'm the one that pushed back on the idea of doing projections at this moment, just due to the volatility of the data we have from the migration.

The mindset being like, I can, you know, we have a variety of methodologies, we apply to projections, that's not a problem, it's more the relative utility of that, given, you know, the volatility of the domain migration.
So we certainly can do that and make sure we're aligned on that and that's the next step I'm happy to take.

Regarding the, I wouldn't, I think the other thing that like concerns me is like, the domain has been up for, like, the domain migration was up for, I guess we did that in, was it early December, right?
And.
It doesn't seem like that was communicated to us, though, from my understanding.
Every other person that's managing a channel for us on the account kind of knew instantly or was taking a look at it or looking at their metrics or something.
It's because everyone's kind of managing to, we're trying to get to a certain level of metrics.
And so I agree that I communicated late in January, like, hey, by the way, we this domain migration.
So I was actually communicating it more on the level of, oh, when we put out content, I know you guys are automating a lot of the content creation.
We should just, like, update the content so that, you know, anything.com is the base domain for interlinking.
Kind of expecting you guys to be like, yeah, we already see it in the data or we are kind of, like, monitoring it because we're paying a lot of attention to the data.
So, yeah, I like...
I'm feeling a little bit of this lack of like metrics drivenness that I kind of felt during the strategy session.
was like at least in the strategy session, it felt like we were like very honed on like, all right, here's the metrics, here's four, we're doing link clicks, it's going to build.
But as I kind of look at like December 1st, December 8th, December 15th, we're kind of like flat.
Maybe that's like publishing.
Like other little pieces of like missing context is like, we had a whole session on like, hey, do we need to be waiting for Kaylee's publishing?
And I kind of said, told the team, no, we don't need to be waiting on Kaylee's publishing, go ahead and publish.
And then that team just like disappeared.
And I think that was, that was Dhruv, a conversation you and I had, I believe it was the second week of December.
And I definitely like can own that there was, you know, some, some disconnected, some loss of knowledge between the strategy sprint and me coming on.
And I know it took us a while to connect.
I think our first call live was that second week.
And that was when I had flagged, like we had.
That that backlog, we were waiting for approvals.
And that's a lot of what we pushed through shortly after that call.
And, you know, and flagging that, again, like, obvious a miss that we could definitely work on improving.
But I know that's something that we did try and prioritize leading into the holidays to, to clear that backlog and make sure that we've got a good cadence.
And we're at this point, we're not waiting for Kaylee's approval.
Like, I share them so that we can have that review.
And then pushing them through anyway, like we had discussed, just in case, you know, not every single article gets eyes on it.
Guys, I'm so sorry.
I need to jump.
I think this, like, went way over.
I, and unfortunately, little behind.
We can follow up on Slack on, on next steps.
Thank you.
All right.
Sounds good.
Thank you.
Thanks.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[To sync on SEO performance and address strategic misalignment.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=1533.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Strategic Misalignment:** A major disconnect exists between GrowthX's "on track" report (275 weekly sessions) and Create's aggressive growth goals, which require SEO to be a primary revenue driver.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=1608.0)
  - [**Domain Migration Conflict:** GrowthX recommends immediate 301 redirects to consolidate SEO authority on `anything.com`. Create is hesitant, fearing a traffic loss from high-ranking legacy domains, citing conflicting advice from advisor Eli Schwartz.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=1326.0)
  - [**Critical Tracking Gap:** Create's Segment/PostHog analytics stack is not integrated with GA4, preventing revenue attribution for organic traffic. GrowthX can consult, but Create must own the technical implementation.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=2229.0)
  - [**Process Breakdown:** Create cited a poor team transition from the strategy sprint to the execution team, leading to lost context, a lack of urgency, and a feeling that GrowthX is not owning the strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=2533.0)

## Topics

### Strategic Misalignment & Growth Goals

  - [Create's primary concern is the slow pace of SEO growth, which is not meeting the urgency required to hit aggressive company revenue goals ($7M ARR → $10M ARR by month-end).](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=1999.0)
  - [The performance report's "on track" status (275 weekly sessions) is seen as insufficient and misaligned with the initial strategy sprint's promise of aggressive growth.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=1999.0)
  - [Create's expectation is for SEO to be a primary revenue driver, not a slow-burn brand-building exercise.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=1800.0)

### Domain Migration Conflict

  - [Create's current setup keeps three domains (`create.xyz`, `createanything.com`, `anything.com`) live to avoid traffic loss from high-ranking legacy pages.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)
  - [GrowthX's recommendation: Implement immediate 301 redirects from legacy domains to `anything.com`.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=845.0)
      - [**Rationale:** The current setup dilutes SEO authority, sends mixed signals to Google, and risks a penalty.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=845.0)
      - [**Risk:** Create fears an immediate traffic hit, citing conflicting advice from advisor Eli Schwartz, who suggested keeping legacy domains live.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=1326.0)

### Performance Tracking & Revenue Attribution

  - [A critical gap prevents tracking organic traffic to revenue.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=2229.0)
  - [**Current Setup:** Create uses Segment → PostHog for product analytics.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=2399.0)
  - [**Problem:** GA4 is not integrated with this stack, making revenue attribution impossible.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=2382.0)
  - [**Ownership:** GrowthX can consult on the tracking strategy, but Create must own the technical implementation.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=2497.0)

### Process & Team Transition Issues

  - [Create cited a poor team transition from the initial strategy sprint (led by Eric) to the current execution team.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=2533.0)
  - [**Impact:** This transition caused lost context, a lack of urgency, and a feeling that GrowthX is not owning the strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=2533.0)
  - [**Specific Examples:**](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=4483.0)
      - [The domain migration, which occurred in early December, was not flagged by GrowthX until Dhruv mentioned it in January.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=4483.0)
      - [A content approval backlog in December was caused by a disconnect between the teams.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=4584.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=4276.0)
      - [Escalate the strategic misalignment internally (e.g., connect Dhruv with Eric).](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=4351.0)
      - [Provide a formal recommendation for the domain migration, including a traffic-loss analysis.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=3113.0)
      - [Develop a more aggressive growth plan, including programmatic SEO opportunities.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=4276.0)
      - [Consult with Create on integrating GA4 with their Segment/PostHog stack for revenue attribution.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=2497.0)
  - [**Create:**](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=2497.0)
      - [Implement the technical instrumentation to connect GA4 with Segment/PostHog.](https://fathom.video/share/Ms45TED55LAfLK7PK7cCC4h9kX1a28tS?tab=summary&timestamp=2497.0)

action_items.description: Reschedule 1:1 w/ Kyle for Jan 9 or Jan 13
Audit GA4/Segment/PostHog; recommend tracking fixes; share w/ Dhruv
Update all new content/sitemaps to anything.com; update internal links in old posts
Draft domain migration plan (301s, GSC, sitemaps, homepage edge); share w/ Dhruv
Escalate internally; schedule call w/ Eric + Dhruv; share aggressive plan + projections
